tour.title: Monorepo Structure & Configuration
tour.description: Start here to understand how the project is organized. The root package.json and pnpm-workspace.yaml define a monorepo with three workspaces: apps/admin (Next.js frontend), packages/service (Hono API), and packages/shared (cross-cutting utilities). Biome handles linting/formatting, and pnpm orchestrates all scripts.

tour.title: Shared Package: Permissions, Roles & Flags
tour.description: Before diving into either the frontend or backend, understand the shared package. It defines the permission constants (what actions exist), role flags (admin vs. regular user), and user flags that both sides consume. This is the contract between the UI and the API.

tour.title: Database Layer: Prisma Models & Seed
tour.description: The data model drives everything. Explore the generated Prisma model files to see all entities (User, Role, Menu, Application, Organization, SystemConfig, AuditLog, etc.) and their relationships. The seed script shows how initial data is created for development.

tour.title: Backend Core: Hono App, Auth & Middleware
tour.description: The service package is a Hono API with OpenAPI support. server.ts creates the standalone HTTP server, app.ts assembles the Hono app with basePath('/api'), and index.ts re-exports for consumption by Next.js. The middleware layer handles authentication sessions, app-context resolution, admin checks, and operation logging.

tour.title: Backend Routes: REST API Resources
tour.description: Each resource (applications, organizations, users, roles, menus, system-config, uploads, logs) has its own route directory with a schema file (Zod validation), individual endpoint files (one per HTTP action), and an index barrel. The routes/index.ts aggregates everything. Study one resource thoroughly (e.g., application/) to understand the pattern, then the rest will be familiar.

tour.title: Frontend Foundation: Layout, Auth & API Client
tour.description: The admin app uses Next.js App Router with a route group (logged) for authenticated pages. The root layout sets up providers, the (logged) layout handles auth redirects and renders the sidebar/frame, and the API client (app-client.ts) uses Hono RPC for type-safe backend calls. The auth-client handles login/register flows via Better Auth.

tour.title: Frontend Pages: CRUD Management Surfaces
tour.description: Each management area (applications, organizations, users, settings, logs) follows a consistent pattern: a page component that fetches data, a table component for listing, a dialog component for create/edit, and a delete confirmation dialog. Study the applications page as a complete example of this CRUD pattern, then see how organizations and users replicate it.

tour.title: Frontend State, Hooks & UI Primitives
tour.description: Zustand stores manage global state (menu tree, system config). Custom hooks provide mobile detection and current-app context. The UI primitives layer is a shadcn/ui-style design system wrapping Radix UI: buttons, dialogs, forms, tables, sidebars, and specialized components like tree-view and draggable-tree for menu management.
